tabled_reason: The FSM 'Setup Data Import from CSV file' path (setupTaskCSVImports) -- the same mechanism that made UnitsOfMeasure pass -- was retried live for tax rates on 2026-07-20 and does NOT create a tax-rate header on this pod. The rate-level setup task ZX_MANAGE_TAX_RATES_AND_TAX_RECOVERY_RATES DOES report ImportSupportedFlag=true and CSVExportImportSupportedFlag=Y, and an import round-trip ran cleanly (HTTP 201, completed), so the earlier note's claim of 'no non-UI path at all' is superseded. BUT: a live setupTaskCSVExports of that task (ESS 9765467) enumerates exactly 22 business objects and NONE of them is a tax-rate-header object -- they are all rate DETAIL/child objects (default tax accounts, amount/unit-price/percent/quantity/gross/withheld schedules and types, recovery types) plus ZX_TAX_STATUS. There is no ZX_RATES / tax-rate-header business object in the task's CSV package, so the CSV import cannot CREATE the base ZX_RATES_B row this fixture needs -- it only round-trips detail attributes onto rates that already exist (rate headers are still created only by the UI-only Tax Configuration Workbook). Two live import attempts (a hand-authored ZX_RATES member, then six candidate header short names ORA_ZX_TAX_RATES/ZX_RATES/ZX_TAX_RATE/ORA_ZX_MANAGE_TAX_RATE/TAX_RATE/ZX_RATES_B) all completed with the CSV silently skipped ('all related CSV files are missing or empty' / unknown-BO no-op) and ZERO rows reached ZX_RATES_B (verified read-only). Additionally the export itself is unreliable here: it takes ~45 min and finishes COMPLETED_ERRORS with the CSV members absent from the returned zip (only the manifest came back), so the exact rate-object CSV column layout could not be learned to attempt a detail-only load. TABLED stands, now with a precise mechanism finding rather than a documentation inference.
task_name: Manage Tax Rates and Tax Recovery Rates

fsm_mechanism_finding.task_discovery: GET setupTasks?q=TaskName LIKE '%Tax Rate%' -> ZX_MANAGE_TAX_RATES_AND_TAX_RECOVERY_RATES; GET setupTaskCSVImports/{task} -> ImportSupportedFlag=true.
export_processes: 100007867615347 (ESS 9765467, full, COMPLETED_ERRORS, no CSV members in zip)
fsm_mechanism_finding.no_rate_header_object: None of the 22 is a tax-rate-header object; the task CSV package carries only rate detail/child objects + tax status. So the FSM CSV import cannot create a ZX_RATES_B row.

import_attempts.outcome: completed, CSV skipped ('all related CSV files are missing or empty'), 0 rows in ZX_RATES_B
each completed HTTP 201, unknown-BO no-op, 0 rows in ZX_RATES_B (prefix 90211)

fsm_mechanism_finding.evidence_file: artifact/EXPORT_MANIFEST_ZX_MANAGE_TAX_RATES.xml (the real live export manifest listing the 22 business objects)

content_upload_limitation: US-only tax-partner content (sales/use tax geography + rates from a registered tax content partner). Cannot create arbitrary custom tax rates.
workbook_ui_action: Rapid Setup Spreadsheets -> Upload Tax Configuration Workbook (select the saved compressed CSV, Upload; monitor on Monitor Upload and Download Processes)

files.purpose: Tax Rates worksheet of the Tax Configuration Workbook (new rate rows attached to an existing regime/tax/status) -- retained for the UI-workbook path; the FSM CSV import does not consume it

discovery.name: US_TAX_REGIME_TAX_STATUS
discovery.sql: SELECT * FROM (SELECT r.tax_regime_code AS RC, t.tax AS TX, s.tax_status_code AS STS FROM zx_regimes_b r JOIN zx_taxes_b t ON t.tax_regime_code = r.tax_regime_code JOIN zx_status_b s ON s.tax = t.tax AND s.tax_regime_code = t.tax_regime_code WHERE r.country_code = 'US' ORDER BY r.tax_regime_code, t.tax) WHERE ROWNUM = 1
discovery.note: Portability rule 7: discover an existing US regime + tax + tax status on the TARGET pod at load time. Returned live 2026-07-20: US EXCISE TAX / US NY EXCISE TAX / US NY EXCISE.

verify.note: ZX_RATES_B is BIP-readable with fin_impl on this pod (confirmed 2026-07-20). Verification is by the prefixed rate code.

base_read.sql: SELECT tax_rate_code AS TAX_RATE_CODE, tax_rate_id AS TAX_RATE_ID, percentage_rate AS PERCENTAGE_RATE FROM zx_rates_b WHERE tax_rate_code LIKE :PREFIX || 'RT-TAXRATE-%'

verify.bad_absence_note: A rate whose parent regime/tax/status does not exist is rejected and never reaches ZX_RATES_B; the bad-row proof is absence from ZX_RATES_B. (Not exercised end-to-end -- no load path creates the good rate either.)
